title: user-api returning 503 errors

alert.title: 🚨 HIGH ERROR RATE: user-api
alert.description: Service 'user-api' error rate spiked to 45%. Multiple 503 responses reported by gateway. Started 12 minutes ago.

user-api.msg: Failed to get DB connection: pool exhausted (active=10, pool_size=10)
Failed to get DB connection: pool exhausted (active=10, pool_size=10)

configs.show_connections:
PID  | STATE  | DURATION
1001 | active | 12m
1002 | active | 11m
1003 | active | 10m
1004 | active | 9m 
1005 | active | 8m
1006 | active | 7m
1007 | active | 6m
1008 | active | 5m
1009 | active | 4m
1010 | active | 3m
(10 rows, all active, none idle)

ground_truth.root_cause: Database connections are never released in DBPool.get_user(). Each request leaks a connection, and with pool_size=10 and max_overflow=0, the pool is exhausted after 10 requests. All subsequent requests fail with ConnectionError.

test_code:
import sys, os
sys.path.insert(0, os.getcwd())
from db.pool import DBPool

def test_connections_released():
    pool = DBPool(pool_size=5)
    for i in range(20):
        user = pool.get_user(i)
        assert user is not None
    assert pool.active <= pool.pool_size

def test_get_user_returns_data():
    pool = DBPool(pool_size=5)
    user = pool.get_user(42)
    assert user['id'] == 42
    assert pool.active <= pool.pool_size

title: Payment service crashing on checkout

alert.title: 🚨 CRASH LOOP: payment-service
alert.description: Service 'payment-service' restarting repeatedly. 12 crashes in last 5 minutes. Users reporting checkout failures.

payment-service.msg: AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'street' in process_payment()
AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'street' in process_payment()

user-service.msg: GET /users/789 returned 200 (user has no address on file)

configs.health_check payment-service:
UNHEALTHY - crash loop detected
Restarts: 12 in 5m

ground_truth.root_cause: In process_payment(), user.get('address') returns None for users who haven't set an address. The code then tries to access address.street on a None object, causing AttributeError crash.

title: Search API extremely slow

alert.title: 🚨 HIGH LATENCY: search-api
alert.description: Search API p99 latency >30s. Users reporting search timeouts. Database CPU at 95%.

search-api.msg: Query took 32451ms: SELECT * FROM products WHERE name LIKE '%shoe%'
Query took 28903ms: SELECT * FROM products WHERE name LIKE '%shirt%'
Deployed version v3.1.0 with new search endpoint

search-db.msg: Slow query detected: full table scan on products (10M rows)

configs.show_config search-db:
max_query_time=60s
max_connections=50
table_sizes:
  products: 10,234,567 rows
  categories: 1,247 rows

ground_truth.root_cause: search_products() executes SELECT * FROM products without a LIMIT clause, causing a full table scan on 10M rows. Every search query scans the entire table.

test_code:
import sys, os
sys.path.insert(0, os.getcwd())
from search.query import SearchEngine

class MockDB:
    def __init__(self):
        self.last_query = ''
    def execute(self, sql):
        self.last_query = sql
        return [{'id': 1, 'name': 'Test Shoe'}]

def test_search_has_limit():
    db = MockDB()
    engine = SearchEngine(db)
    engine.search_products('shoe')
    assert 'LIMIT' in db.last_query.upper(), 'Query must have a LIMIT clause'

def test_search_returns_results():
    db = MockDB()
    engine = SearchEngine(db)
    results = engine.search_products('shoe')
    assert len(results) > 0

title: Cache hit rate dropped to 0%

alert.title: 🚨 CACHE MISS RATE 100%: product-cache
alert.description: Cache hit rate dropped to 0% after last deploy. Database load increased 10x. Response times degrading.

product-service.msg: Cache TTL check: ttl=3600 > max_ttl=3600, skipping cache write
Cache TTL check: ttl=3600 > max_ttl=3600, skipping cache write
Deployed version v4.2.0 with cache optimization

ground_truth.root_cause: In CacheManager.set(), the condition uses `>` instead of `>=`. Since default ttl (3600) equals max_ttl (3600), the condition ttl > max_ttl is False, so cache writes are never skipped — wait, actually the bug is the opposite: the intent was to reject TTLs ABOVE max, but since ttl=max_ttl, it passes. Actually re-reading: the condition says if ttl > max_ttl: return (skip), and ttl=3600 == max_ttl=3600 means 3600 > 3600 is False, so it should NOT skip... Let me re-check the logs. The logs say 'ttl=3600 > max_ttl=3600, skipping cache write'. So the code in the logs contradicts the source — the bug in the source is that it uses > when the deploy changed max_ttl. The real bug: the operator should be <= for allowing, the code skips when ttl > max_ttl, but previous version had max_ttl=7200. The deploy changed max_ttl to 3600 to match ttl, but the code checks >, so 3600 > 3600 is false, meaning it SHOULD write. But logs say skipping. Ok let me simplify: the bug is the operator is wrong — it should be `>` to reject too-large TTLs but actually the condition should allow equal. I'll fix the source to make the bug clearly `>=` (rejects equal).

title: Deploy pipeline failing

alert.title: 🚨 DEPLOY FAILED: notification-service
alert.description: Deployment of notification-service stuck in 'pending' state for 20 minutes. Rollback not triggered. Previous version still running.

notification-service.msg: Current version v2.0.3 still running (no new deploy received)

deploy-controller.msg: Failed to parse config/deploy.yaml: yaml.scanner.ScannerError: mapping values are not allowed here, line 8, column 12
Deploy status set to 'pending' (no rollback configured for config errors)

source_files.config/deploy.yaml:
service: notification-service
version: v2.1.0
replicas: 3
resources:
  cpu: 500m
  memory: 256Mi
env:
  LOG_LEVEL:DEBUG
  DB_HOST: db.internal
  REDIS_URL: redis://cache:6379
health_check:
  path: /health
  interval: 30s

configs.show_recent_deploys:
v2.1.0  2026-04-03T15:40:00Z  notification-service  PENDING (failed)
v2.0.3  2026-04-01T12:00:00Z  notification-service  ACTIVE

ground_truth.root_cause: In config/deploy.yaml, line 8 has 'LOG_LEVEL:DEBUG' which is missing a space after the colon. YAML requires 'key: value' format. Should be 'LOG_LEVEL: DEBUG'.

test_code:
import sys, os
sys.path.insert(0, os.getcwd())
import yaml

def test_yaml_parses():
    with open('config/deploy.yaml') as f:
        config = yaml.safe_load(f)
    assert isinstance(config, dict), 'Config must be valid YAML dict'

def test_env_vars_parsed():
    with open('config/deploy.yaml') as f:
        config = yaml.safe_load(f)
    env = config.get('env', {})
    assert 'LOG_LEVEL' in env, 'LOG_LEVEL must be a key in env'
    assert env['LOG_LEVEL'] == 'DEBUG'

solution_patch.config/deploy.yaml:
service: notification-service
version: v2.1.0
replicas: 3
resources:
  cpu: 500m
  memory: 256Mi
env:
  LOG_LEVEL: DEBUG
  DB_HOST: db.internal
  REDIS_URL: redis://cache:6379
health_check:
  path: /health
  interval: 30s
